text: Robotic assisted left adrenalectomy. The patient is a fifty-five year old male with past medical history of refractory hypertension. As part of a workup, an abdominal CT was performed that revealed one point six centimeters adrenal lesion. A bilateral renal-venous sampling was made showing a highly increased rate of aldosterone production on the left adrenal gland.
The surgeon moves the spleen from a lateral to medial position and identifies and mobilizes the tail of the pancreas to gain access to the adrenal gland.
The surgeon separates the fat tissue from the right crus, identifies the adrenal gland, and proceeds with its dissection and mobilization. The adrenal gland is resected from lateral to medial.

The surgeon ligates all pedicles during the dissection until the left adrenal vein is identified and clipped. Following successful hemostasis, the adrenal gland specimen is placed into an endopouch for retrieval.
Operative time was ninety minutes, blood loss was minimal and the patient was discharged the following day. The pathologist informed a cortical anadroma that measured one point six centimeters.
